note: Commentary and evaluative labels removed from raw data. This content is interpretive and belongs in analysis, not in raw data inputs.

removed_items.original_value: Small gap: GDP slightly exceeds GNI, indicating modest net foreign capital presence. Much smaller than Ireland or Luxembourg distortions.
Relative poverty = share below 50% of median equivalised disposable income.
fragmented — strong in some areas, nearly absent in others
Well below OECD average. Declining for decades. Most recent WVS wave estimate.
Well below OECD average. Declining for decades. 'Most people can be trusted' response has fallen from ~50% in 1970s.
~1 in 4 women experience intimate partner violence. ~1 in 9 men. Gun violence in domestic contexts is uniquely American problem.
mixed — high divorce rate, high single-parent household rate (particularly in Black and Hispanic communities, reflecting structural economic pressures)
moderate — formal volunteering declining, informal mutual aid rose during COVID but receded
growing in crisis moments but not institutionalized
Catastrophic decline from ~35% in 1950s. Private sector unionization ~6%. Collective bargaining coverage ~12%.
moderate but declining
US Surgeon General declared loneliness an epidemic in 2023. ~50% of adults report measurable loneliness.
Top 1% holds 30.5% of wealth. Bottom 50% holds 2.5%. The ratio is ~12:1 between median and mean wealth — extreme inequality. During COVID, billionaire wealth increased 70%.
Among lowest in OECD (average ~34%). The US collects far less in taxes than peer nations, limiting redistribution capacity.
No national VAT. State sales taxes range 0-10.25%. Regressive by nature.
HIGHEST in world by GDP share but with worst outcomes among developed nations. Most spending is private, not public.
Well below OECD average (~18%). Minimal public investment in social protection relative to peers.
Among lowest social mobility in developed world. Parent income is highly predictive of child income. The 'American Dream' is statistically more achievable in most European countries.
Virtually zero public cultural spending at federal level. National Endowment for the Arts budget is ~$200M — negligible.
minimal domestically, but documented cases (Jan 6 defendants, whistleblowers like Reality Winner)
severely restricted — Dobbs decision overturned federal abortion rights; state-level restrictions vary wildly
highest incarceration rate in the world (~2M people)
Black Americans incarcerated at 5x rate of White Americans. This is a core integrity violation under GST framework.
declining — politicized Supreme Court appointments, erosion of public trust
formally strong but structurally unequal — public defenders overworked, bail system criminalizes poverty, racial disparities at every stage
CRITICAL GST CONCERN: The Alabama example. A Black mother of 2 in rural Alabama and a Wall Street lawyer have vastly different access to justice. This is exactly the gap the GST measures.
thin — unemployment benefits time-limited, no universal healthcare, no paid parental leave nationally, no guaranteed housing
declining — tightening asylum rules, 3.6M pending immigration cases, detention conditions criticized
NOT universal — this alone is a major compassion deficit
CRISIS — record homelessness, severe rent burden, no right to housing
~12% of households food insecure
formally universal but severely unequal by district funding (property tax-based), student debt crisis
declining sharply
mixed — strong FOIA law but extensive classification, corporate secrecy, and lobbying opacity
declining — political interference in science agencies, corporate capture of research
not state censorship but corporate platform moderation and algorithmic suppression
weak — at-will employment in most states, no guaranteed paid leave, minimal collective bargaining
present but fragile — grew during COVID, receding since
varies enormously by geography and income

original_value.note: GST analysis to be performed separately from data collection
